instructions: DO NOT execute any destructive or potentially destructive commands against the production database — SELECT only, no exceptions.
Don't access live production databases — use local copies for analysis when possible.
AWS CLI may hang — ask user to perform operations via CloudShell when it does.
All environment modifications must go through CDK code and cdk deploy, not through AWS Console or CLI.
Application config (ALLOWED_HOSTS, ENV_URL, etc.) goes through Secrets Manager, not EB environment properties. EB env vars are bootstrap-only.
The test database name is nexus, not nexusmvp. Always use database='nexus' in pymysql connections.
Test data must be synthetic (purpose-built for test environment), not copied from production.
Service table column is title, not name or service_name.
DB tables use PascalCase (e.g., Service, ServiceOffering, not service, service_offering).
Present proposed data changes (descriptions, content updates) for managing developer approval before applying to the database.
Use py (not python3) to run Python scripts on this machine.

When adding or switching an active workstream on an existing ARAS subsystem (without removing the current task), update Current Task on all affected ARA.md surfaces to show Active and On Hold tasks; update Exclusive Scope (remove paths moving to shared), Shared Scope (add new paths with sharing partners named), and Shared-Scope Interactions to match — applying identical changes to both the module surface (.claude/.aras/[branch]-surface/ARA.md) and the branch surface (aras-surface/[branch]-surface/ARA.md) for every affected subsystem. Co-participant surfaces must also be updated to reclassify previously exclusive paths as shared with the new partner.
